Culture Manager Internal/Policy HR-03 January 1, 2026 1.12 • Revision of Paternity Leave, Marriage Leave, Bereavement Leave Senior People & Culture Manager Internal/Policy HR-03 3 | P a g e 1. Purpose and Applicability This policy encourages employees to take a break from work and unwind, as it helps ensure a healthy and efficient staff. The Leave Policy sets out various types of leaves that an employee is eligible for and outlines the procedures for taking leave. This policy is applicable to all Azilen permanent employees. 2. Types of Leave The company follows the calendar year (January to December) for all aspects related to the leave policy. All leaves are credited at the beginning of each Calendar year. Our current leave policy allows 18 paid offs to all employees, as followed. Type of Leaves Total Leaves Details Casual Leave (CL) 6 1) All CL can be consumed or will be en -cashed by the end date of December month of every year. Sick Leave (SL) 6 1) SL cannot be encashed or carried -forward. It shall lapse at the end of the year. 2) In case of having SL for more than 3 days, a valid medical certificate shall be

en -cashed by the end date of December month of every year. Sick Leave (SL) 6 1) SL cannot be encashed or carried -forward. It shall lapse at the end of the year. 2) In case of having SL for more than 3 days, a valid medical certificate shall be presented to the HR. Privilege Leave (PL) 6 1) For unused PL balance at the end of the year, an employee can opt any of the following options: • Leave Encashment • Carry -Forward 2) A maximum cumulative PL balance of up -to 20 Leaves shall be considered for carry -forward at the end of the year. Any additional PLs will be processed for encashment by default. 3) Employee shall apply PL in 5 working days advance. Maternity Leave 26 weeks 1) 1) Employees who have been in continuous employment of the Company for not less than 80 days 4 | P a g e prior to the expected date of delivery will be allowed maternity leave on gross pay for up to 26 weeks, of which a maximum of 8 weeks could be availed before delivery. An approved medical practitioner should certify the confinement and she must not take

| P a g e prior to the expected date of delivery will be allowed maternity leave on gross pay for up to 26 weeks, of which a maximum of 8 weeks could be availed before delivery. An approved medical practitioner should certify the confinement and she must not take up any employment, temporary or part -time, or otherwise, during these 26 weeks. 2) For women who are expecting after having 2 children, the duration of paid maternity leave shall be 12 weeks (i.e., 6 weeks pre and 6 weeks post expected date of delivery). 3) Maternity leave of 12 weeks to be available to mothers adopting a child below the age of three months from the date of adoption as well as to the “commissioning mothers”. The commissioning mother is defined as biological mother who uses egg to create embryo planted in any other woman. 4) The provision relating to "work from home" for women, which may be exercised after the expiry of the 26 weeks' leave period. Depending upon the nature of work, women employees may be able to avail this benefit on terms that are mutually agreed with the emp loyer. 5) In case of miscarriage or

to "work from home" for women, which may be exercised after the expiry of the 26 weeks' leave period. Depending upon the nature of work, women employees may be able to avail this benefit on terms that are mutually agreed with the emp loyer. 5) In case of miscarriage or medical termination of pregnancy, a worker is entitled to six weeks of paid maternity leave. Bereavement Leaves 2 1) This leave is effective from March 1, 2021 2) This leave is applicable in case of demise of any immediate family member 3) Immediate family member includes: Parents, In- laws parents, Siblings, Spouse, Child 4) This will lapse at the end of the year & cannot be encashed. 5) As it is special leave, the right of adding this leave balance lies with HR. Employee needs to inform HR for these leaves to be added in their leave balance. 5 | P a g e Paternity Leave 2 1) This leave can be availed following the birth of the child. 2) This leave should be availed within one week of the child’s birth. 3) This is a continuous leave, available twice in your tenure with Azilen . 4) As it is

P a g e Paternity Leave 2 1) This leave can be availed following the birth of the child. 2) This leave should be availed within one week of the child’s birth. 3) This is a continuous leave, available twice in your tenure with Azilen . 4) As it is special leave, reach out to HR whenever the benefits to be availed. Marriage Leave 2 1) This leave can be availed on the occasion of your marriage 2) It has to be availed consecutively 3) Your marriage day should be included in the leave Gift Leave 3 1) This can be availed if all the leave balance is exhausted but in requirement of leave due to exigent circumstance 2) Exigent circumstance includes medical attention required for you or an immediate family member: Parents, In -laws parents, Siblings, Spouse, Child 4) This can be gifted or can be taken as gift up to 3 days in a calendar year 5) As it is special leave, reach out to HR whenever the benefits to be availed 6) This leave cannot be extended or encashed 6 | P a g e 3. up to 3 days in a calendar year 5) As it is special leave, reach out to HR whenever the benefits to be availed 6) This leave cannot be extended or encashed 6 | P a g e 3. Leave Encashment 3.1. Guidelines: 3.1.1. All encashment or carry -forward balances will be considered as of the last day of every year (31st Dec). 3.1.2. Basic Salary would be considered for the purpose of the leave encashment. 3.1.3. Any employee taking an exit during the year will not be eligible for Leave Encashment. 3.1.4. The employee must be an active & not resigned at the time of leave encashment disbursement. 4. General Rules 4.1. All the leaves should be applied in HRIS. 4.2. In case of new entrants into the company, all leaves will be calculated proportionately. 4.3. All leaves except for sick leaves should be informed in advance. 4.4. For leaves that have taken due to medical reasons, the approving authority or HR may ask for documentary proof validating the medical condition including examination by the company doctor at the discretion of the company. This may be requested even after t he leave is availed, regardless of approval. Documents submitted

leaves that have taken due to medical reasons, the approving authority or HR may ask for documentary proof validating the medical condition including examination by the company doctor at the discretion of the company. This may be requested even after t he leave is availed, regardless of approval. Documents submitted must be with the substantial proof(s), failure to submit the required documents will be treated as Leave without Pay 4.5. No leave shall be availed without the approval from the respective authority. The Employee shall be entitled to all the above leave in accordance with the Company's HR policy as amended from time to time 4.6. During the notice period , employee can use their earned leaves without extension of notice period, basis manager’s approval. Any unearned leaves used by employee will be adjusted against the F& F. The earned leaves shall be calculated category wise on pro -rata basis, until the last working day for the resigned employee. 4.7. The employee should apply leave(s) in advance and necessary approval should be obtained before availing the leaves. If leaves are not approved, it would be treated as an unauthorized absence.
